ogTitle: Fishermans Reach vs Fishing Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fishermans Reach and Fishing Point.
heading: Fishermans Reach vs Fishing Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fishermans Reach, New South Wales and Fishing Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fishing Point has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Fishermans Reach (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fishermans Reach or Fishing Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fishermans Reach has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Fishing Point scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fishermans Reach scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Fishing Point scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fishermans Reach or Fishing Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fishing Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.62% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
